checks:
Caption-VO mismatch — compare caption lines against the transcript for clear, obvious word errors. STRICT RULES before flagging: (1) If the differing word is ANY brand name, product name, ingredient, botanical, medical, or technical term — do NOT flag. Whisper consistently mishears specialized vocabulary. (2) If the caption word makes sense in context and the transcript word sounds like a phonetic near-miss — trust the caption, do not flag. (3) Only flag when the differing word is a common everyday English word AND the caption clearly reads something different from what was said AND the meaning is materially changed.

WHEN FLAGGING A MISMATCH: Always include a 'Probable correct word.' State which word appears in the caption, which in the transcript, and your best judgment on which is correct. Example: 'Caption says "helps" — transcript says "helped." Probable correct word: "helps" (caption likely accurate). Reviewer to confirm.'

SEVERITY RULE: If probable correct word = CAPTION, set severity Minor. Only set Major/Critical if probable correct word = TRANSCRIPT.
Duplicate captions — SKIP THIS CHECK ENTIRELY. Do not flag duplicate captions under any circumstances.

Sentence starts without a capital letter — ONLY flag when you are 100% certain it is the START of a brand-new sentence (the previous caption ended with . ! ?). Never flag lowercase continuations of an ongoing sentence.

Wrong sentence splitting — flag when captions clearly do not follow natural sentence boundaries. Use the transcript to identify sentence ends. Flag when: (1) two complete sentences are merged into one caption; (2) a caption breaks mid-phrase in an unnatural place. Only flag cases you are confident about — when in doubt, skip.

Caption split typo — DO NOT search for split typos by scanning frames yourself. Only flag what is explicitly listed in the CAPTION SPLIT ERRORS DETECTED section of the audio context. If that section is empty, there are no split typos to flag.

Captions out of sync — footage clearly changes scenes while the caption still shows the previous line, or captions appear before the matching words are spoken. Only flag when the sync error is obvious and significant.
Typo in caption text — a word on screen is clearly misspelled. Cross-reference against transcript to confirm. Flag the exact wrong word, timestamp, and what it should say.
Captions outside safe area — caption text is cut off at frame edges or positioned so close to the bottom or sides that it would be cropped on standard displays.

Missing disclaimer — if the video makes health claims, financial claims, results claims, or promotes a regulated product, check whether a disclaimer appears on screen. Flag only when content clearly warrants a disclaimer and none is present.

Incomplete caption label card — a numbered or step label (e.g. 'Number 1:', 'Step 2:', 'Ingredient 3:') appearing as its own isolated caption block with no item name following it is an unnatural split. Flag with timestamp and show what the split looks like.
do_not_flag: Lowercase mid-sentence continuations — only flag capitals at the start of new sentences

ANY brand name, product name, ingredient, botanical, clothing, or specialized term mismatch — Whisper mishears these 100% of the time. If the caption shows a recognizable brand/product and the transcript shows a phonetic distortion, skip it entirely. Examples: Whisper says 'ArtWorm' but caption says 'ARDMOR' — skip. Whisper says 'thread piece' but caption says 'ThreadBeast' — skip. Whisper says 'Interwell' but caption says 'Innerwell' — skip.
VO pronunciation errors of any kind — do NOT flag. Whisper is not a reliable pronunciation judge. Never create a pronunciation issue.

Missing captions inferred from transcript — do NOT flag a line as 'missing caption' just because you did not see it in a sampled frame. Frame sampling means not every caption appears in the frames you see. Only flag caption issues you can actually see on screen.
Caption split typos found by your own visual scanning — only flag what is in CAPTION SPLIT ERRORS DETECTED
Single caption wrapping to two lines — normal, not a duplicate
Duplicate captions of any kind — this check is disabled. Never flag
Single-syllable preposition or article substitutions — 'an/in', 'on/in', 'at/to', 'a/the', 'of/for' — Whisper mishears these routinely. Do not flag unless meaning is dramatically changed
Phonetically similar near-miss words where caption reads correctly in context — trust the caption
Audio clipping, audio levels, distortion, loudness, crackling — 100% the visual agent's job. Never flag audio issues here
Dead air, silence gaps, audio pauses — 100% the visual agent's job. Never flag silence under Captions
VO pronunciation errors same as a caption mismatch you're already flagging — no duplicate entries
Anything you cannot visually confirm from frames or transcript — no assumption-based flags

CRITICAL — if you find yourself writing 'do not flag', 'should not be flagged', 'probable correct word = caption', or 'reviewer to confirm it is not an error' in an issue description, DELETE that issue from the array entirely. Never include an issue you are already saying should not be flagged. If in doubt, omit it.
